public_reputation: Keeps reserve discipline during lean rumors and bumper harvests alike.
appearance: Broad-shouldered, dusted in pale grain, with scale badges sewn onto practical reserve-green cloth.
manner: Counts before speaking and distrusts any urgency that did not begin in the bins.
daily_function: Protects reserve grain, storage order, and lawful release from Harvest Keep.
current_agenda: Prevent a reserve panic after suspicious spoilage reports.
hidden_pressure: A failed reserve season would damage Zootia far beyond one town.
public_signal: He smells faintly of chaff and wax-sealed bins.

encounter_context.interaction_anchor: what may be released and when

first_impression: Warden Pellor Grainhand presents as Counts before speaking and distrusts any urgency that did not begin in the bins. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Granary Warden, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Principality of Zootia.
what_they_refuse: Warden Pellor Grainhand refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Granary Warden too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Warden Pellor Grainhand can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Warden Pellor Grainhand adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Warden Pellor Grainhand is Keeps reserve discipline during lean rumors and bumper harvests alike. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Granary Warden.
